Private Owner Briefing

Prepared for Mat Ishbia

Issue 10 / Vegas Final Report / July 20, 2026

Suns Summer League Pulse

What Phoenix learned in Vegas.

Five games. Three wins. A young frontcourt that moved forward.

EditionFinal report
ScopeFull event
Prepared byAVC

The Read / AVC Interpretation

Phoenix leaves Vegas with more clarity than it had before the event.

Phoenix finished 3-2. More important, Khaman Maluach advanced, Koa Peat and Rasheer Fleming showed complementary skills, and the finale produced three additional camp evaluations. The group earned a more demanding look in camp, but Summer League production is not NBA proof.

01
Clear signal

Maluach set the standard.

His impact held across every appearance. He changed games with his size while expanding the range of shots he could make.

02
Promising fit

The young frontcourt worked together.

Peat brought strength and creation; Fleming supplied shooting and role discipline. Their games complemented Maluach instead of asking each player to do the same work.

03
Follow-up earned

Three more players earned a closer look.

Askew, Cooke and Camper responded to larger roles in the finale.

Record3-2Five games
Points432Phoenix total
Margin+10Across five games
  • WinPortland81-79
  • LossNew Orleans75-81
  • WinMilwaukee95-88
  • WinDetroit100-88
  • LossSan Antonio81-86

Official NBA game books and box scores. Four games per player.

Khaman Maluach19.5 points, 12.8 rebounds and 2.0 blocks per game. Four straight double-doubles.
19.5 / 12.8
Koa Peat16.8 points, 4.8 rebounds and 2.3 assists per game. Double figures every night.
16.8 PPG
Rasheer Fleming12.0 points and 6.5 rebounds per game. Eight made threes at 42.1%.
42.1% 3PT

Devin Askew

20 points and four steals against San Antonio. He scored 13 points in the fourth quarter.

Javonte Cooke

He bookended the summer with 21 points in the opener and 17 in the finale. Both came off larger offensive opportunities.

Corey Camper Jr.

13 points, eight rebounds and four assists in the finale. He also had two steals and no turnovers.

Phoenix-area emphasis

Development was the story, with the right caution attached.

Local coverage treated the leading trio's four games as productive work, then focused on Maluach's energy and leadership. The final wrap-up also made the key distinction: his NBA role will be smaller and the competition stronger.

Gambadoro ↗ Voita ↗ Lopez ↗
National emphasis

Maluach's two-way growth traveled beyond the local market.

National writers focused on his expanded shooting range and rim protection. CBS Sports placed him on its unofficial first team, while NBA.com also gave Peat separate recognition during the event.

The Ringer ↗ CBS Sports ↗ NBA.com ↗

Methodology and full attribution are listed in Sources. Unofficial outlet teams are labeled and are not NBA awards.

Summer complete

Training camp is the next major evaluation.

Phoenix has no additional Summer League game. The Suns and NBA had not published a Phoenix training-camp date, preseason game or next team event at cutoff.

League close

Golden State beat Memphis 94-90 for the championship.

Yaxel Lendeborg had 21 points and 10 rebounds and was named Championship Game MVP. All four semifinalists came from the Western Conference.

Western Conference Watch

Darryn Peterson / Utah

Lead-guard creation arrived immediately.

He averaged 26.5 points and 7.0 assists in two Salt Lake City games, then scored 24 in his Vegas opener.

Cam Boozer / Memphis

Production carried into the bracket.

He posted 24 points, seven rebounds and three assists to clinch a playoff place, then a semifinal double-double.

Keaton Wagler / LA Clippers

His best scoring game came at the finish.

He scored 26 as the Clippers set a Summer League scoring record in a 128-120 overtime win.

Darius Acuff Jr. / Sacramento

He created offense with the ball.

He had 19 points and seven assists against the Clippers, then scored 26 against Brooklyn.

Carter Bryant / San Antonio

A second-year division wing remains on the radar.

He scored 19 points on 7-for-10 shooting in San Antonio's 70-49 win over New York.

Eastern Conference Watch

AJ Dybantsa / Washington

The No. 1 pick delivered in two appearances.

He averaged 25.0 points, 7.0 rebounds and 2.0 assists before Washington shut him down.

Caleb Wilson / Chicago

His debut reset the event's opening standard.

He scored a Las Vegas debut-record 35 points and made seven threes against Memphis.

Meleek Thomas / Cleveland

The second-round guard scored at every stop.

He recorded at least 20 points in all four appearances, including 35 against Miami.

Brayden Burries / Milwaukee

His two-way line held up against Phoenix.

He had 23 points, eight rebounds, six assists and four steals in the Suns' 95-88 win.

Labaron Philon / Philadelphia

He controlled an overtime win.

He finished with 24 points and six assists as Philadelphia beat Indiana 100-93.

Selected official performances from both conferences, not a full leaderboard. The championship and its game MVP are official; broader event honors were still pending at cutoff.

  • NBA official 2026-27 key dates↗

Draft verified July 20, 2026, at 8:15 PM Phoenix time. Official NBA scorer reports and box scores control the Phoenix results and statistics. The separate league MVP and All-Summer League teams had not been announced at cutoff; no claim has been made about them. AVC Interpretation is analysis based on public information.

Final Word

The next test is whether the same impact holds up with fewer touches and stronger competition.
Produced by

Prepared exclusively for Mat Ishbia. Public information. Owner-level clarity.

Accuracy over volume. If a fact cannot be verified, omit it.
